title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Demott Craddock interviews Eleanor Jelinsky regarding Marina Gregg’s mental state. Jelinsky explains that Marina is prone to extreme emotional shifts and is highly sensitive to topics involving children or mental institutions. She notes that Marina’s only child, whom she had with her ex-husband Isidore Wright, is "a bit crazy," and the memory of this child haunts her. Jelinsky adds that Marina’s conflicts typically stem from jealousy, suspicion, or contract issues, though she does not seem to fear any specific person.

Later, at the Blue Wild Boar pub, Craddock meets with Detective William "Tom" Tidler. Tidler reports that while movie studio staff find Marina annoying, they consider her charming, and they view her husband, Jason Radcliffe, as a great producer or director. Craddock expresses suspicion regarding a lethal drug that Marina believes was intended for her, noting that Marina only informed her doctor of the matter rather than her husband. Tidler provides details on Marina’s four past marriages: her first was to a realtor, her second to a foreign count or prince, her third to an actor named Robert Trask, and her fourth to the playwright Isidore Wright.

Craddock decides to consult Miss Marple, an astute elderly woman living near the church, to help identify "suspect X" from a group of twenty people. Meanwhile, Miss Marple visits Mrs. Jamison at the "Diane's Stylist" hair salon to request old issues of movie magazines to assist in her own investigation.
